preamble: The transformation from an alveolar flap to a voiced uvular fricative is a process of uvularization, often referred to as the 'guttural R' shift, where the place of articulation for rhotics moves from the front to the back of the vocal tract.

examples.note: The historical alveolar trill or flap shifted to a uvular fricative or trill starting in 17th-century Paris.
Standard German shifted from an alveolar trill to a uvular realization in most dialects.
The strong R (word-initial or double rr) has shifted to uvular or glottal positions in many urban dialects.

sources: Lodge, R. A. (1993). French: From Dialect to Standard.
Wiese, R. (1996). The Phonology of German.
